book_claims.claim: Richest food source of selenium, a powerful antioxidant mineral preventing free radical tissue damage
book_claims.mechanism: 6 nuts supply ~780% RDA selenium. Selenium is cofactor for glutathione peroxidase, a key antioxidant enzyme.

supplementary_research.finding: RCT (Thomson et al., 2008) found that a single serving of Brazil nuts (2 nuts) raised plasma selenium levels as effectively as 100mcg selenomethionine supplement, with effects lasting at least 12 weeks and also improving glutathione peroxidase activity.

Clinical trial found that just one Brazil nut daily for 3 months significantly improved selenium status, reduced inflammatory markers (IL-6, TNF-alpha), and improved lipid profile (LDL reduction) in obese women (Cominetti et al., 2012).

Meta-analysis (Rayman, 2012; updated) found optimal selenium status associated with reduced risk of thyroid disease, certain cancers (prostate, lung, colorectal), and cognitive decline, though excessive intake (>400mcg/day) may increase type 2 diabetes risk.

culinary_pairings.source: Selenium + flavanols for combined antioxidant protection
Selenium + anthocyanins complement antioxidant defense systems
Morning smoothie or porridge topping for daily selenium
Traditional pairing; both high in minerals
Smoothie base; potassium + selenium for combined mineral benefit

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Predominantly as selenomethionine, the most bioavailable form. Just 2 nuts provide ~100% RDA. Excess is excreted but chronic overconsumption (>400mcg/day) risks selenosis.
Among the highest of any nut. Supports hundreds of enzymatic reactions including energy metabolism and muscle function.
Polyphenol with demonstrated anticarcinogenic and anti-inflammatory properties; converted to urolithins by gut bacteria.
